report_name: Prior Authorization Metrics for Medical Items and Services (Excluding Drugs)
reporting_entities: State Medicaid and Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP) fee-for-service (FFS) programs
Qualified Health Plan (QHP) issuers on the Federally Facilitated Exchanges (FFEs)
metrics: List of all medical items and services that require prior authorization
Standard PA requests: Percentage approved in the calendar year
Standard PA requests: Percentage denied in the calendar year
Standard PA requests: Percentage approved in the calendar year after appeal
Standard PA requests: Average (mean) response time
Standard PA requests: Median response time
Expedited PA requests: Percentage approved in the calendar year
Expedited PA requests: Percentage denied in the calendar year
Expedited PA requests: Percentage approved in the calendar year after appeal (optional)
Expedited PA requests: Average (mean) response time
Expedited PA requests: Median response time
Percentage of requests where the timeframe for review was extended and the request was approved
Standard PA requests: (Optional) Percentage approved within 7 days
Standard PA requests: (Optional) Percentage denied within 7 days
Standard PA requests: (Optional) Percentage denied after time for review was extended
Standard PA requests: (Optional) Percentage denied after appeal
Expedited PA requests: (Optional) Percentage approved within 72 hours
Expedited PA requests: (Optional) Percentage denied within 72 hours
Expedited PA requests: (Optional) Percentage denied after time for review was extended
Expedited PA requests: (Optional) Percentage denied after appeal
dimensions: Line of business (MA contract level, state level for FFS, plan level for managed care, issuer level for QHP)
filters: Aggregated for all items and services (for percentages and response times)
business_rules: Response times are measured from the time the payer receives the request, not when it is sent by the provider.
Percentage approved after appeal (standard) must be a subset of the total number of standard prior authorization requests appealed.
Percentage approved after appeal (expedited) must be a subset of the total number of expedited prior authorization requests appealed.

Prior to January 1, 2026, decision timeframes: MA plans/integrated plans (72 hrs expedited, 14 days standard); State CHIP FFS (14 days standard); Medicaid managed care/CHIP managed care (72 hrs expedited, 14 days standard); QHP issuers on FFEs (72 hrs expedited, 15 days standard).
Beginning January 1, 2026, decision timeframes: 72 hours for expedited requests, 7 calendar days for standard requests.
Percentages should be calculated using 'total requests' or 'total appeals' as the denominator, as appropriate.
exclusions: Drugs are excluded from the list of medical items and services requiring prior authorization.
Medicaid FFS programs had no required decision timeframes for either request type prior to January 1, 2026.
CHIP FFS programs had no required decision timeframes for expedited prior authorization requests prior to January 1, 2026.
reporting_frequency: Annually, starting in 2026, for the previous calendar year
notes: Payers must publicly report metrics on their websites.
Encouraged to present metrics in a clear, visual format (e.g., bar charts, pie charts).
Highly recommended to report both counts and percentages.
Optional to differentiate between standard and expedited requests for the 'extended timeframe and approved' metric.
Template provided is not required but encouraged.
Public reporting promotes transparency and accountability, helps patients understand prior authorization processes, and enables providers to evaluate payer performance.
Metrics can be used to compare plans, programs, and payers.
Contact information should be provided for questions on the data.
